opp.name: Santam S2C
opp.next_step: Feb 20, 2026: POC in progress Jan 22, 2026: POC is being planned, Caroline proposed an onsite meeting while in SA On Nov 25, 2025 Santam shared their NDA and Menachem has forwarded it to our legal team for their review Today Nov 27, Menachem sent a follow up email to Legal requesting feedback Next steps: Following the NDA review, we'll proceed to book a follow up discussion with the prospect.
opp.ais_why:
• Mar 2026 Avoma notes focus on a custom claims-excess processing application built on the Zycus platform; AI is not a topic in any captured conversation. Deal scope includes Merlin alongside eProcurement, iContract, and iSupplier ‚Äî AI products are present (Merlin) but not driving conversation. The functional discussion is anchored in insurance-claims-specific workflow configuration.
• Formal Evaluation with BD Engaged and very high meeting cadence (14 meetings Oct 2025 - Mar 2026) with stable Santam stakeholders (Daphney Mvelase, Mpfuxi Makondo, Ashlee Naidoo, Hendrico Kokum, Zodwa Moshanyana) and active Zycus team. The meeting topics centre on functional gaps (claims excess processing) rather than AI use cases. Last activity Mar 31, 2026.
• Medium confidence: clear meeting evidence (14 calls) but topic centers on functional/workflow configuration rather than AI. Merlin in scope is not enough to push above 5/6 boundary without buyer-spoken AI language. Default DOWN at 5/6 held; would move UP if Merlin/POC discussion surfaces in later meetings or if a Phase 2 emerges with AI-specific scope.

sources: SF Opportunity Description: empty
SF Next Step: POC references (Feb 20, Jan 22, 2026) but no business-case context
Avoma: zero calls discovered
Contact roles: 8 identified, including exec and procurement leads, but no engagement record
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI has been documented in any Salesforce field or call transcript. The opportunity Description field is empty, and synthesis-map fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c) returned no data or do not exist in this org. No Avoma calls were discovered to extract buyer-stated pain or impact metrics. The Next Step references a POC planned and launched in early 2026, but without recorded discovery or scoping calls, the business problem the POC was meant to validate remains unanchored. For a $200K S2C deal (iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin) the buyer would typically articulate savings targets (contract leakage reduction, supplier onboarding cycle time, maverick spend capture) or efficiency gains (FTE reduction in procurement ops), but none of that signal exists in the record. The immediate move is to requalify the deal by engaging the executive sponsor (Johan Human, Executive Head: Group Sourcing & Transformation) and the procurement leadership (Aslam Higgins, Head of Procurement; Marcel Rose, Procurement Manager) to surface and quantify the pain that justified the POC, then anchor the business case in writing.

sources: SF Contact Roles: 8 contacts, no Champion role assigned
SF Next Step: Marcel Rose mentioned in NDA and POC context, but no advocacy evidence
Avoma: zero calls discovered, no champion behavior recorded
Last activity: 2026-03-31, 93 days ago, no recent champion engagement
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. The synthesis-map fields (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Champion_Identified__c) either do not exist or returned no values. The Opportunity Contact Role list includes eight contacts, but none are flagged with a Champion role, and there is no evidence in Next Step, tasks, or Avoma calls of any contact advocating for Zycus, making introductions to the economic buyer or decision maker, sharing internal docs, or driving the agenda. Marcel Rose (Procurement Manager) is the most frequently mentioned contact in tasks and Next Step, which suggests he may have been a primary point of contact during the POC planning phase, but there is no evidence of champion behavior—no record of him pushing back on competitors, no evidence of him facilitating access to Johan Human or Aslam Higgins, and no sign of active engagement since March 31, 2026 (93 days ago). The 93-day silence across all contacts means any early-stage champion relationship has gone cold. The gap: we do not know who, if anyone, internally at Santam is pushing for Zycus, what their motivation is, or whether they have influence over the decision. The move is to requalify by re-engaging Marcel Rose and Aslam Higgins to assess their disposition, then identify and cultivate a champion with access to the executive sponsor (Johan Human) and the budget authority.

sources: SF Competitors__c: unknown
Avoma: zero calls discovered
SF Next Step: no competitor references
Synthesis-map competitive fields: no data returned
competition.narrative: No competitor has been identified in any source. The Competitors__c field is unknown (either does not exist in this org or was not populated). The synthesis-map fields that would carry competitive signal (Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Moved_To__c, Closed_Lost_Reason_Code__c) either do not exist or returned no values. No Avoma calls were discovered, so there are no buyer quotes like 'we are also looking at Coupa' or 'we are benchmarking against Ariba.' The Next Step and task history do not reference any competitor demos, RFP rounds, or shortlist comparisons. This is a red flag for a deal at 267 days old with a POC launched in February 2026: by this stage, a buyer running a competitive eval would typically have engaged multiple vendors, and the lack of any competitive intel suggests either (a) Santam is sole-sourcing Zycus (unlikely without a strong champion or incumbent relationship, neither of which is evident), (b) the competitive landscape was never discovered during the POC phase (a qualification gap), or (c) the deal has stalled so early that Santam has not yet run a formal multi-vendor process. The gap is a knowledge gap—we genuinely do not know the competitive context—and it is a critical blind spot. The move is to requalify by asking Marcel Rose, Aslam Higgins, or Johan Human directly whether they are evaluating other vendors, what their current stack is (SAP, Oracle, Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, Jaggaer are common in large SA enterprises), and how Zycus is being benchmarked.

sources: SF Opportunity Description: empty
Avoma: zero calls discovered
Synthesis-map pain fields: no data returned
Products scope: iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin (implies S2C pain, but not confirmed)
identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer in any recorded source. The synthesis-map fields that would carry pain (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c) either do not exist in this org or returned no values. The Opportunity Description is empty. No Avoma calls were discovered, so there are no buyer quotes describing what is broken today, what the cost of inaction is, or what problem the POC was meant to solve. The product scope (iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin) implies Santam is addressing source-to-contract transformation—likely pain around contract compliance, supplier onboarding inefficiency, maverick spend, or lack of AI-driven automation in procurement—but this is inference, not confirmed pain. Without a named pain owner (e.g., Aslam Higgins owning maverick-spend reduction, or Johan Human owning procurement-transformation KPIs) and without a quantified impact (cycle time, cost, risk), we cannot anchor the value proposition or the urgency. The move is to requalify by asking the procurement team and exec sponsor to describe the current-state problem, the pain owner, and the cost of not solving it, then document that in writing.

sources: SF Next Step: NDA shared Nov 25, 2025; legal review requested Nov 27, 2025
Avoma: zero calls discovered, no contracting or InfoSec discussions recorded
Synthesis-map fields: no data on legal, InfoSec, or partner involvement
paper_process.narrative: The Next Step documents that Santam shared their NDA on Nov 25, 2025, and Zycus legal was engaged for review, with a follow-up sent on Nov 27, 2025. This indicates Santam has a formal legal/contracting gate early in their process, which is common for large enterprises and suggests they will require MSA/SOW negotiation, potentially InfoSec review, and likely procurement-channel approvals before contracting. However, there is no record of the NDA review outcome, whether it was signed, or whether it unblocked the POC or any subsequent technical engagement. The paper process from here to close is undocumented: no evidence of MSA/SOW scoping, no InfoSec or data-privacy review mentioned (critical for a SaaS procurement platform handling supplier and contract data), no mention of implementation partner or SI involvement (though a $200K S2C deal in South Africa might involve a local partner for deployment). The Current_Contract_Expiration__c field (if it exists) would indicate whether Santam has an incumbent contract creating a forcing function, but that field returned no data. The gap: we know the early legal gate (NDA) but not the contracting path, the InfoSec requirements, or the partner model. The move is to requalify by asking procurement (Marcel Rose) or the exec sponsor (Johan Human) what the remaining legal/security/procurement gates are, what the NDA outcome was, and whether an SI or partner is required for the implementation.

sources: SF Contact Role: Johan Human (Executive Head: Group Sourcing & Transformation)
SF Contact Role: Aslam Higgins (Head of Procurement)

Avoma: zero calls discovered

economic_buyer.narrative: The stakeholder map includes Johan Human (Executive Head: Group Sourcing & Transformation), a title that typically signals budget authority for enterprise procurement transformation initiatives, and he holds an Opportunity Contact Role, indicating he was engaged at some point. However, there is no evidence in Next Step, tasks, or Avoma transcripts of Johan participating in commercial or pricing discussions, nor any record of his active sponsorship since the deal was created in October 2025. Aslam Higgins (Head of Procurement) is also a contact role and would likely co-own or influence the business case and budget, but similarly no engagement is recorded. The synthesis-map fields for economic buyer identification (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) either do not exist in this org or returned no values. The gap: we have plausible EB candidates by title, but zero evidence of their involvement in the commercial motion, and the 93-day silence means we cannot confirm whether they are still engaged, whether budget is secured, or whether the deal has executive cover. The move is to re-engage Johan Human directly (executive connect) to confirm budget ownership, timeline, and sponsorship, and to surface whether the POC outcome has been reviewed at his level.

sources: SF Contact Roles: 8 contacts, exec + procurement + influencers, but engagement sequence unclear
SF Next Step: NDA (Nov 25, 2025), POC planning (Jan 22, 2026), POC in progress (Feb 20, 2026)
Avoma: zero calls discovered, so no call-sequence process map available
Last activity: 2026-03-31, 93 days ago, process appears stalled
decision_process.narrative: The stakeholder structure suggests a multi-layer process: procurement leadership (Aslam Higgins, Head of Procurement; Marcel Rose, Procurement Manager) would drive the functional evaluation, Johan Human (Executive Head: Group Sourcing & Transformation) would provide executive sponsorship and budget approval, and additional influencers (Jared Sukhdeo, Matthew Ballard, Ntombizodwa Moshanyana, Daphney Mvelase, Lylle Cornelius) are mapped but their roles in the decision are unclear (several have 'NA' titles in contact roles). The Next Step records an NDA shared by Santam on Nov 25, 2025, forwarded to Zycus legal for review, with a follow-up on Nov 27, 2025, which indicates procurement was driving the process at that point and a legal/contracting gate existed before deeper engagement. The POC planning (Jan 22, 2026) and launch (Feb 20, 2026) suggest the process moved from contracting readiness to technical validation, which is typical for a Qualified-to-Formal-Evaluation transition. However, the process has stalled: no activity since March 31, 2026, no record of POC completion, no evidence of a scoring meeting, shortlist decision, or next-gate trigger. The approval chain (Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) is undocumented. The gap: we know the early-stage process (NDA, POC kickoff) but not the path from here to close—how many approvals, what gates remain, who scores the POC, what triggers Shortlisted or Vendor Selected stage, and whether a partner or SI is involved. The move is to re-engage and ask Marcel Rose or Aslam Higgins to walk through the remaining steps, timeline, and approvers.

sources: SF Next Step: POC references (Jan 22, Feb 20, 2026) but no success criteria documented
SF Products: iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin (scope is clear, criteria are not)
Avoma: zero calls discovered
Synthesis-map fields: no data returned
decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria, RFP, RFI, shortlist requirements, or scorecard have been documented. The synthesis-map fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c) either do not exist in this org or returned no values. The Next Step indicates a POC was planned (Jan 22, 2026) and in progress (Feb 20, 2026), which implies Santam defined success criteria for the POC, but those criteria are not recorded anywhere—neither in Salesforce free-text fields nor in any call notes (zero Avoma calls discovered). A POC without documented success metrics is a red flag: we do not know what Santam was testing, what outcomes would have moved them to Shortlisted or Vendor Selected, or whether the POC passed, failed, or stalled. The product scope (iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin) suggests a full S2C suite evaluation, and Merlin's inclusion indicates AI/automation appetite, but without recorded buyer priorities (e.g., contract compliance vs. supplier collaboration vs. spend analytics vs. negotiation automation) we cannot tailor positioning. The move is to requalify by asking the procurement team (Marcel Rose, Aslam Higgins) and the executive sponsor (Johan Human) what the POC was meant to prove, what the decision criteria are for vendor selection, and what the current evaluation status is.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — Merlin is included in the product scope, which signals that Santam expressed interest in AI-driven procurement automation at some point (likely during initial scoping or POC planning). However, AIS fields are unpopulated, and no calls were recorded to capture buyer-stated AI appetite, use cases, or urgency. Without this signal, we cannot confirm whether Santam is AI Hungry (seeking AI as a core capability, willing to pay for it, and using it as a decision criterion), AI Curious (interested but not urgent, treating AI as a differentiator rather than a requirement), or whether the AI interest has cooled. The POC scope (if it included Merlin) would be the test: if Santam validated AI-driven negotiation or sourcing agents during the POC and found value, they are likely AI Hungry; if Merlin was not part of the POC or the outcome is unknown, AI fit is unconfirmed. Requalification must surface this.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — Merlin is in product scope, indicating Santam has AI/automation appetite for procurement transformation. However, AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) returned no data, so AI excitement level is unknown. No Avoma calls discovered to assess whether the Merlin/agentic narrative is landing or whether AI is a differentiator in the POC. With no recorded discovery or demo calls, AI positioning strength cannot be assessed. This is a requalification priority: if Merlin is part of the POC scope, we need to confirm whether Santam sees AI-driven negotiation and sourcing automation as a core decision criterion or a nice-to-have.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 8 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 0 is carried by exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15); commercial strength +0.40 (weight 5). Offsetting it: momentum 29 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 29 (slipping backward): Engagement cadence dropped (pulse dark). Eased toward flat — 93d quiet (>30d window); momentum down 22pt (stalling).
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.06).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 25 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 3 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (0), nudged by commitment 25 and momentum 29, and dragged by risk 8. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

Without this signal, we cannot confirm whether Santam is AI Hungry (seeking AI as a core capability, willing to pay for it, and using it as a decision criterion), AI Curious (interested but not urgent, treating AI as a differentiator rather than a requirement), or whether the AI interest has cooled. The POC scope (if it included Merlin) would be the test: if Santam validated AI-driven negotiation or sourcing agents during the POC and found value, they are likely AI Hungry; if Merlin was not part of the POC or the outcome is unknown, AI fit is unconfirmed. deal_movement.summary: Deal created Oct 9, 2025. NDA shared by Santam and routed to Zycus legal Nov 25-27, 2025. POC planning discussed Jan 22, 2026, with Caroline proposing an onsite meeting. POC marked 'in progress' Feb 20, 2026. Last Salesforce activity March 31, 2026. No activity for 93 days. No stage progression from Qualified. No field history of stage, amount, or forecast changes captured, suggesting static deal mechanics since creation.

items.detail: Deal is 93 days dark with 90 days to close. POC outcome is unknown, and no progression from Qualified stage has occurred. To close by Sept 30, 2026, Santam would need to complete POC validation, move to Shortlisted, run commercials, negotiate, contract, and deploy in 12 weeks—unrealistic without immediate re-engagement and compression.

Johan Human (Executive Head: Group Sourcing & Transformation) is a contact role, indicating exec-level interest at deal creation, but no evidence of his active sponsorship, budget approval, or engagement in the POC phase. Without exec alignment, the deal lacks the air cover required to drive urgency, secure budget, and navigate procurement/legal gates. Risk: the deal was initiated by procurement (Marcel Rose) without exec buy-in, and the 93-day silence may indicate loss of exec priority.

Product scope is a full S2C suite (iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin), which implies replacing or augmenting Santam's existing procurement stack and requires change management across sourcing, procurement ops, supplier management, and contract management teams. No Avoma calls or Salesforce fields document the current-state stack, the scope of displacement, end-user readiness, training requirements, or executive commitment to the transformation. Without a change-management plan and exec-driven adoption strategy, deployment risk is high.

Santam shared their NDA on Nov 25, 2025, and Zycus legal was engaged for review on Nov 27, 2025. The Next Step indicates that 'following the NDA review, we'll proceed to book a follow up discussion with the prospect,' but there is no record of the review outcome, whether the NDA was signed, or whether it unblocked the POC. If the NDA was never resolved, the POC may have been informal or the legal gate may still be blocking progression. If it was resolved, the lack of documentation is a hygiene gap. Either way, this is a vulnerability: legal/contracting clarity is missing, and without MSA/SOW visibility, the path to close is unclear.

POC outcome is unknown. The Next Step indicates the POC was 'in progress' as of Feb 20, 2026, and Caroline proposed an onsite meeting on Jan 22, 2026. However, there is no record of POC completion, scoring, pass/fail assessment, or buyer feedback. The last activity date is March 31, 2026, which suggests engagement continued for about 6 weeks after the POC launch, but then went dark. Without knowing whether the POC validated the business case, met success criteria, or surfaced gaps, we cannot assess win probability or next steps. This is the single highest vulnerability: the deal is frozen at 'POC in progress' with no resolution for 93 days.

Marcel Rose (Procurement Manager) was the primary point of contact during NDA review and POC planning phases (Nov 2025 - Feb 2026), but there is no evidence of champion behavior: no advocacy, no facilitation of access to the economic buyer (Johan Human) or decision maker (Aslam Higgins), no competitive push-back, and no active engagement for 93 days. Any early-stage relationship has gone cold.

items.commitment: Complete legal review of Santam NDA and provide feedback to enable follow-up discussion with prospect
Book follow-up discussion with Santam following NDA review completion
Complete POC evaluation and provide feedback on validation outcomes

items.action: Executive connect: the deal owner's manager to engage Johan Human (Executive Head: Group Sourcing & Transformation) directly to requalify the deal—confirm whether the POC validated the business case, whether Santam intends to proceed, what the timeline and remaining gates are, and whether executive sponsorship and budget remain committed. This is the single highest-leverage move to determine whether the deal is recoverable or should be closed lost.
items.trigger: Deal has been dark for 93 days (last activity 2026-03-31), POC outcome is unknown since Feb 20, 2026, and close date is 90 days out with no momentum. Johan Human is the exec sponsor contact role but has no visible engagement.
items.expected_effect: Requalifies the deal by surfacing current buyer intent, POC outcome, timeline, and exec commitment. If positive, reopens the relationship and creates a path to Shortlisted/Vendor Selected. If negative, enables clean close-lost decision and resource reallocation.

items.action: Deal team: re-engage Marcel Rose (Procurement Manager) and Aslam Higgins (Head of Procurement) via multi-channel outreach (email + LinkedIn + phone) to surface POC feedback, understand what caused the 93-day silence, and assess whether procurement still owns the initiative or whether it has been deprioritized. Offer a POC debrief call and a value-add follow-up (e.g., benchmarking data, ROI model, reference customer in financial services or insurance vertical).
items.trigger: Marcel Rose was the primary contact during NDA and POC phases (Nov 2025 - Feb 2026) but has been silent since March 31, 2026. No response to rep outreach is visible in Next Step.
items.expected_effect: Reopens the procurement-level relationship and surfaces the reason for the stall (POC outcome, budget, competing priorities, internal politics). If Marcel/Aslam are still engaged, this creates a bridge to the exec connect. If they are unresponsive, it confirms the deal is dead and should be closed lost.

items.action: Deal team: if the POC outcome is negative or unknown after requalification, propose a compressed re-scoping: a focused use-case demo or mini-POC targeting the highest-value pain point (e.g., contract compliance automation via iContract, or AI-driven negotiation via Merlin) with a 2-week validation window and documented success criteria. Position this as a fast path to decision and offer to bring in a reference customer from the insurance or financial services vertical to de-risk the evaluation.
items.trigger: POC marked 'in progress' Feb 20, 2026, but outcome is unknown and no progression occurred. If requalification surfaces that the POC stalled due to scope ambiguity, lack of exec alignment, or unclear success criteria, a re-scoped validation is the recovery path.
items.expected_effect: Provides a clear, time-bound path to validation and decision. Mitigates the risk of an inconclusive or failed POC by narrowing scope and increasing exec visibility. If Santam agrees, this moves the deal to the relevant stakeholder/Shortlisted and compresses the timeline.

items.action: Deal team: surface and document the competitive landscape during requalification. Ask Marcel Rose, Aslam Higgins, or Johan Human directly whether Santam is evaluating other vendors, what their current procurement stack is (SAP, Oracle, Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, Jaggaer are common in large South African enterprises), and how Zycus is being benchmarked. If a competitor is named, immediately run a competitive positioning review with the deal team and prepare a wedge strategy (e.g., Merlin AI negotiation agent as a differentiator vs. legacy platforms).
items.trigger: No competitor identified in any source (Competitors__c unknown, no Avoma calls, no Next Step references). For a 267-day-old deal with a POC, the lack of competitive intel is a critical blind spot.
items.expected_effect: Uncovers the competitive context and enables tailored positioning. If Santam is sole-sourcing Zycus, this confirms strong preference and shortens the sales cycle. If they are multi-vendor, this surfaces the wedge and the competitive moves required to win.

items.action: Deal team: if requalification confirms buyer intent and the deal is recoverable, secure a champion within the procurement or sourcing team (ideally Marcel Rose or a peer with access to Johan Human and Aslam Higgins) by offering to co-build the internal business case, provide ROI modeling and benchmarking data, and facilitate a reference call with a Zycus customer in a similar vertical (insurance, financial services, or large SA enterprise). Position the champion as the internal advocate who can drive urgency, navigate approvals, and push back on competing priorities.
items.trigger: No champion identified. Marcel Rose was the primary contact but showed no advocacy behavior and has been silent for 93 days. Without a champion, the deal lacks internal momentum and is vulnerable to deprioritization.
items.expected_effect: Builds internal advocacy and accelerates the decision process. A champion with exec access can drive urgency, secure budget approval, and navigate legal/procurement gates, increasing win probability and compressing timeline.

items.signal: No buyer engagement for 93 days (last activity 2026-03-31)
items.interpretation: Deal is dark. No two-way engagement, no inbound from buyer, no meetings, no emails visible in Next Step or tasks since March.

items.signal: POC marked 'in progress' Feb 20, 2026, but no record of completion or outcome for 133 days
items.interpretation: POC has either stalled, failed, been deprioritized, or was never formally closed out. This blocks progression and signals loss of urgency or buyer disengagement.

items.signal: No Avoma calls discovered for this opportunity or account in trailing 12 months
items.interpretation: Either (a) calls occurred but were not recorded/transcribed, (b) engagement was email/task-only (unusual for a $200K enterprise POC), or (c) minimal substantive buyer engagement ever occurred. All three are red flags.

items.signal: Stage has remained Qualified since creation (Oct 9, 2025, 267 days ago) with no progression to Formal Evaluation, Shortlisted, or Vendor Selected
items.interpretation: No formal advancement despite POC launch. Suggests POC did not validate progression criteria or buyer has not moved the deal forward internally.

items.signal: Single-threaded: Marcel Rose (Procurement Manager) is only visible contact in Next Step and tasks; no evidence of exec (Johan Human) or Head of Procurement (Aslam Higgins) engagement
items.interpretation: Lack of multi-threading and exec alignment means deal is vulnerable to Marcel's departure, deprioritization, or loss of internal sponsorship.

items.signal: Close date is 90 days out (Sept 30, 2026) with deal in Qualified stage and zero momentum
items.interpretation: Close date is unrealistic. To hit it, Santam would need to validate POC, shortlist, run commercials, negotiate, contract, and deploy in 12 weeks—impossible from a cold start.

confidence_signals.summary: All momentum signals are negative. No buyer engagement for 93 days. POC outcome unknown. No stage progression from Qualified. No calls recorded. Single-threaded with no exec or multi-contact engagement visible. No champion, no EB engagement, no business case, no competitive intel, no decision criteria documented. Every MEDDPICC element is either a gap or partial with no recent confirmation. The deal is cold and requires executive-led requalification to determine whether it can be revived or should be moved to Closed Lost / Omitted.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-10-09, 267 days old. Stage: Qualified (early). Days to close: 90. Time in Qualified: unknown qualified date, but last activity 93 days ago. POC launch referenced Feb 20, 2026 in Next Step but no evidence of completion or progression since March. No field history for stage transitions, suggesting deal has remained in Qualified throughout. With 90 days to a $200K close and zero buyer contact for three months, required pace is aggressive requalification and compressed eval/negotiation cycle within 12 weeks—currently unachievable without immediate re-engagement.
evidence: Next Step last updated with POC in progress Feb 20, 2026, but no subsequent progression
No Avoma calls discovered for this opportunity or account in trailing 12 months
No contact role engagement visible since March
Close date 2026-09-30 (90 days) requires immediate requalification but deal has been dark for entire Q2 2026
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal is cold: 93 days since last buyer engagement (2026-03-31), POC stalled since February, and close date now 90 days out with no momentum to validate readiness.

flags: DARK: No buyer engagement for 93 days (last activity 2026-03-31). Deal is cold and requires immediate re-engagement.

SINGLE-THREADED: Marcel Rose (Procurement Manager) is the only contact with visible engagement in Next Step and tasks. No evidence of multi-threading to exec sponsor (Johan Human) or Head of Procurement (Aslam Higgins) since deal creation.

POC OUTCOME UNKNOWN: POC marked 'in progress' Feb 20, 2026, but no record of completion, scoring, pass/fail, or next steps. This blocks progression to Shortlisted/Vendor Selected and is a requalification priority.
NO CHAMPION: No champion identified or developed. Marcel Rose shows no advocacy behavior, and 93-day silence suggests any early relationship has cooled.
NO ECONOMIC BUYER ENGAGEMENT: Johan Human (Executive Head) is a contact role but no evidence of his involvement in commercial discussions or active sponsorship.
NO BUSINESS CASE: No quantified metrics, ROI, or pain documented. Cannot defend value proposition or urgency without this.
NO COMPETITIVE INTEL: Competitive landscape is unknown for a 267-day-old deal with a POC. Critical blind spot.

FORECAST INDEFENSIBLE: Pipeline forecast with 90 days to close, no buyer engagement for 93 days, Qualified stage (early), no EB/champion/metrics/criteria documented, and POC outcome unknown. Should be Omitted.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is dark (93 days since last buyer engagement), single-threaded (no multi-contact engagement visible), MEDDPICC coverage is near-zero across all elements, POC outcome is unknown, and close date is 90 days out with no momentum. Immediate executive-led requalification is required.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor identified in any source. This is a knowledge gap: the competitive landscape is unknown, which is a critical blind spot for a 267-day-old deal with a POC launched in February. The lack of competitive intel suggests either sole-sourcing (unlikely without strong champion evidence), a qualification gap during POC phase, or the deal stalled before a formal multi-vendor eval. Immediate requalification is required to discover whether Santam is benchmarking other vendors (SAP, Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, Jaggaer are common in large SA enterprises) and how Zycus is positioned.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin is in product scope, indicating Santam has AI/automation appetite for procurement transformation. However, AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) returned no data, so AI excitement level is unknown. No Avoma calls discovered to assess whether the Merlin/agentic narrative is landing or whether AI is a differentiator in the POC. With no recorded discovery or demo calls, AI positioning strength cannot be assessed. This is a requalification priority: if Merlin is part of the POC scope, we need to confirm whether Santam sees AI-driven negotiation and sourcing automation as a core decision criterion or a nice-to-have.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Product scope is clear (iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin), indicating a full S2C suite with AI/automation appetite (Merlin). However, buyer expectations, success criteria, decision criteria, and POC validation metrics are entirely undocumented. No RFP, RFI, scorecard, or recorded requirements exist. Without knowing what Santam prioritizes (contract compliance, supplier collaboration, spend analytics, negotiation automation, AI-driven sourcing) or what the POC was meant to prove, fit assessment is impossible. This is a requalification priority.

hard.ais_why:
• Mar 2026 Avoma notes focus on a custom claims-excess processing application built on the Zycus platform; AI is not a topic in any captured conversation. Deal scope includes Merlin alongside eProcurement, iContract, and iSupplier ‚Äî AI products are present (Merlin) but not driving conversation. The functional discussion is anchored in insurance-claims-specific workflow configuration.
• Formal Evaluation with BD Engaged and very high meeting cadence (14 meetings Oct 2025 - Mar 2026) with stable Santam stakeholders (Daphney Mvelase, Mpfuxi Makondo, Ashlee Naidoo, Hendrico Kokum, Zodwa Moshanyana) and active Zycus team. The meeting topics centre on functional gaps (claims excess processing) rather than AI use cases. Last activity Mar 31, 2026.
• Medium confidence: clear meeting evidence (14 calls) but topic centers on functional/workflow configuration rather than AI. Merlin in scope is not enough to push above 5/6 boundary without buyer-spoken AI language. Default DOWN at 5/6 held; would move UP if Merlin/POC discussion surfaces in later meetings or if a Phase 2 emerges with AI-specific scope.

deltas.subject: Santam shared their NDA on Nov 25, 2025, and Zycus legal was engaged for review on Nov 27, 2025. The Next Step indicates that 'following the NDA review, we'll proceed to book a follow up discussion with the prospect,' but there is no record of the review outcome, whether the NDA was signed, or whether it unblocked the POC. If the NDA was never resolved, the POC may have been informal or the legal gate may still be blocking progression. If it was resolved, the lack of documentation is a hygiene gap. Either way, this is a vulnerability: legal/contracting clarity is missing, and without MSA/SOW visibility, the path to close is unclear.

scope

gaps: Zero Avoma calls discovered via both opp-id (006P700000OIJw1IAH, 15-char: 006P700000OIJw1) and account-id pull, despite 8 contact roles and 267-day deal age. Either calls were not recorded/transcribed, engagement was email/task-only, or Avoma association failed. This is a critical evidence gap affecting all MEDDPICC analysis.

No field history returned for stage transitions, amount changes, or forecast changes, suggesting either deal has remained static since creation or field history is not retained/accessible in this org.

Synthesis-map MEDDPICC fields (Metrics, Economic Buyer, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Pain, Champion, Competition) either do not exist in this org or returned no values, removing a major source for reconstructing buyer engagement and qualification depth.
AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) returned no data, so AI excitement level cannot be assessed despite Merlin in product scope.

No tasks or events were returned in the 90-day query window, despite Next Step referencing POC planning (Jan 22, 2026) and NDA follow-up (Nov 27, 2025). This suggests task/event data is incomplete or the query failed.
Competitors__c field is marked 'unknown,' indicating either the field does not exist in this org or was never populated. No competitive intel exists in any source.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Formal Evaluation')
